steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The subject is Food writer J. Kenji Lopez-Alt — one person, singular. The relative clause "who explores the science behind cooking" is parenthetical; removing it leaves: "Food writer J. Kenji Lopez-Alt ______ that lower boiling points... can wreak all sorts of havoc."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: explains — third-person singular (-s ending). "J. Kenji Lopez-Alt explains" — subject and verb agree. Correct.

B: are explaining — plural auxiliary "are." One person cannot "are explaining." Eliminated.

C: explain — plural base form. "J. Kenji Lopez-Alt explain" — number mismatch for a singular subject. Eliminated.

D: have explained — plural auxiliary "have." Same number error as B and C. Eliminated.
